data.title: U.K Competition Watchdog Probes Safran, Collins Aerospace Deal
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

The U.K competition watchdog launched an investigation into French aerospace-industry supplier Safran's proposed purchase of a part of Collins Aerospace's actuation and flight control business.

The Competition and Markets Authority said Tuesday that it was considering whether the merger, if carried into effect, would result in a lessening of competition within any market in the U.K. for goods or services.

The regulator set a deadline of April 16 for its phase 1 decision.

Safran couldn't immediately be reached for comment.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: Sterling Rises to Two-Week High Against Euro After U.K. Jobs Data — Market Talk
data.description: Sterling rises to a two-week high against the euro and trims losses versus the dollar after data showed the U.K. unemployment rate unexpectedly held steady and wages accelerated in the three months to December. The jobless rate remained at 4.4% compared with the 4.5% expected in a WSJ survey of economists. Average earnings, excluding bonuses, rose 5.9% on year after a 5.6% rise in the previous three-month period, as expected. This is "incompatible with a sustainable return to the Bank of England's 2% inflation target over the medium term," Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown says in a note. EUR/GBP falls to a low of 0.8293 after the data from 0.8303 beforehand. GBP/USD rises to 1.2623 from 1.2601. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: InterContinental Hotels Group Lifts Dividend After Rise in Revenue
data.description:
(07:02 GMT) *InterContinental Hotels Group Raises Dividend to 114.4c
(07:02 GMT) *InterContinental Hotels Group Final 2024 Dividend a 10% Raise from 2023
(07:04 GMT) *InterContinental Hotels Group 2024 Rev $4.92B
(07:04 GMT) *InterContinental Hotels Group 2024 Rev Grew 6%
(07:05 GMT) *InterContinental Hotels Group 2024 Oper Pft $1.04B
(07:06 GMT) *InterContinental Hotels Group 2024 Net Pft $628M
(07:08 GMT) *InterContinental Hotels Group 2024 Room Revenue Accelerated in 4Q
(07:11 GMT) *InterContinental Hotels Group Lauches New $900m Buyback Program
(07:27 GMT) InterContinental Hotels Group Lifts Dividend After Rise in Revenue

By Pierre Bertrand

InterContinental Hotels Group raised its dividend for last year after an increase in revenue.

The London-listed group said Tuesday that it made $628 million in net profit for the year, compared with $750 million a year prior, on revenue that grew 6.5% to $4.92 billion.

Room revenue, a key metric for the hotel industry, accelerated in the fourth quarter of the year, rising to 3.0% for the whole year as room occupancy remained largely flat and average rates edged 2.1% higher on year, the owner of the Holiday Inn, Crowne Plaza and Regent hotel brands said.

Revenue from reportable segments--the company's preferred metric, which includes fee business and owned, leased and managed lease hotels--came to $2.31 billion. Company-compiled consensus was for $2.32 billion.

The company said that it would propose a 114.4 cents final dividend, for 2024, 10% higher than prior year's. It added that it would launch a $900 million share buyback program this year, which, when combined with dividends is expected to return more than $1.1 billion to shareholders this year.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: Antofagasta Reports Lower Attributable Profit for FY24, Revenue Grows
data.description:
Antofagasta said Tuesday its profit for 2024 declined, while revenue climbed year over year.

Profit attributable to owners of the parent for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was $829.4 million, compared with $835.1 million earlier. EPS moved to $0.841 from $0.847.

The UK-listed Chilean mining company's revenue was $6.61 billion, compared with $6.32 billion a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a final dividend of $0.235 per share for the period, payable May 12 to shareholders on record April 22. It paid $0.243 per share a year ago. This takes the full-year dividend to $0.314 per share, down from $0.36 in the previous year.

data.title: InterContinental Hotels Buys Ruby Brand for Up to $305.7 Million
data.description:
(07:01 GMT) *InterContinental Hotels Acquires Ruby
(07:02 GMT) *InterContinental Hotels Targets Global Expansion
(07:03 GMT) *InterContinental Hotels Buys Ruby, Related IP, for Initial EUR110.5M
(07:28 GMT) InterContinental Hotels Buys Ruby Brand for Up to $305.7 Million

By Ian Walker

InterContinental Hotels Group is buying the Ruby hotel brand for up to 291.5 million euros ($305.7 million) as part of the Crown Plaza and Iberostar owner's expansion plan.

The London-listed hotel company said it would pay an initial sum of 110.5 million euros for the Ruby brand and intellectual property, with a further amount of up to 181 million euros between 2030 and 2035 depending upon certain targets being met.

Ruby brand currently operates 20 hotels, consisting of 3,483 rooms, in major cities across Europe and has another 10 pipeline hotels, equivalent to 2,235 rooms.

IHG said it expects to start integrating the Ruby hotels into its system later this year and complete it by March 31, 2026.

"We see excellent opportunities to not only expand Ruby's strong European base but also rapidly take this exciting brand to the Americas and across Asia, as we have successfully done with previous brand acquisitions," IHG Hotels & Resorts Chief Executive Elie Maalouf said.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: Tinc Eyes EUR61 Million Investment in Dutch Battery Storage Project
data.description:
Tinc plans to invest 61 million euros in Project Mufasa, a battery energy storage system in North Sea Port Vlissingen, Netherlands.

The Belgian infrastructure investor is one of the project's private partners and will own a 36.67% stake following its investment, according to a Tuesday release. It will fund the project under its 200 million-euro revolving credit facility.

Project Mufasa is scheduled to be operational in the first half of 2027. The initiative aims to strengthen critical energy infrastructure, supporting the Netherlands' renewable energy transition and powering over 200,000 households with its 350-megawatt battery system.

data.title: Mining Giant BHP Cuts Dividend as Weak Commodity Prices Weigh on Interim Earnings
data.description:
BHP Group (, BHG.JO) slashed its dividend for the fiscal first half after weaker commodity prices and global economic headwinds drove its underlying attributable profit down 23% year over year.

For the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, the mining giant reported an underlying attributable profit of $5.08 billion, down from $6.57 billion a year earlier due to exceptional losses. However, profit attributable to shareholders soared to $4.42 billion from $927 million, buoyed by disciplined cost controls amid the softer pricing environment, according to a Tuesday release.

The company also highlighted improving conditions in China, where copper demand exceeded expectations. BHP anticipates further growth in Chinese copper demand, driven by government stimulus focused on power infrastructure and consumer durables.

"The strength of the result demonstrates BHP's operational resilience and its ability to perform through the cycle, with standout production performances in the half from Escondida, [Western Australia Iron Ore] and [BHP Mitsubishi Alliance]," Chief Executive Officer Mike Henry said. "The demand for BHP products remains strong despite global economic and trade uncertainties, with early signs of recovery in China,

resilient economic performance in the US and strong growth in India," he added.

Still, revenue slipped to $25.18 billion from $27.23 billion, despite higher sales volumes of key commodities such as copper, iron ore, and steelmaking coal. The decline was largely attributed to lower realized prices for iron ore and steelmaking coal, partially offset by stronger copper prices.

Against this backdrop, BHP's board declared an interim dividend of $0.50 per share, down from $0.72 per share a year earlier. The payout is scheduled for March 27 to shareholders on record March 7.

Looking ahead, the miner cautioned that labor market tightness will continue to pressure the mining industry's cost base in 2025, despite easing inflation across its key operating regions.

data.title: Anglo American Sells Nickel Business for Up to $500 Million
data.description:
By Michael Susin

Anglo American said it agreed to sell its nickel business to a subsidiary of Chinese government-backed mining company MMG for up to $500 million in cash.

The London-listed miner said Tuesday that the deal comprises an upfront cash payment of $350 million at completion, with the remaining consideration subject to certain milestones and investment decisions.

Anglo's nickel business comprises two ferronickel operations and two growth projects in Brazil.

"The sale of our nickel business after a highly competitive process marks a further important milestone towards simplifying our portfolio to create a more highly valued copper, premium iron ore, and crop nutrients business," Chief Executive Duncan Wanblad said.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: InterContinental Hotels Launches $900 Million Share Buyback Program
data.description:
InterContinental Hotels Group launched a share buyback program of up to $900 million, according to a Tuesday filing.

The buyback will reduce the hospitality company's issued share capital and allow it to return surplus capital to shareholders.

The scheme will run until Dec. 29, with all the shares repurchased earmarked for cancelation.

data.title: Beijing Digital Telecom Invests 190 Million Yuan in Structured Deposit Products
data.description:
Beijing Digital Telecom unit Zhuhai Dixintong Technology subscribed to 190 million yuan worth of structured deposit products from Bank of Communications and Industrial Bank.

The mobile device retailer said it used its idle funds for the investment, which has a term of 182 days with an annualized floating return rate of between 1.50% and 2.15%, according to a Monday filing.

data.title: Gillette India shares skyrocket 13% after Q3 earnings
data.description:
Shares of Gillette India soared over 13 percent on February 18 as investors cheered for the company's Q3 earnings. The personal care company's net profit for the December quarter surged over 21 percent on year to Rs 125.97 crore, up from Rs 103.95 crore in the same period last fiscal.

Revenue also grew to Rs 685.55 crore in Q3, up 7.2 percent from the Rs 639.46 crore that it reported in the year ago period.

At 12.44 pm, shares of Gillette India were trading at Rs 8,307.80 on the NSE. The sharp uptick in the stock was further fuelled by a spike in trading volumes in the counter. As much as seven lakh shares of the company changed hands on the exchanges thus far, a meteoric surge from the one-month daily traded average of 28,000 shares.(This is a developing story, please come back for more.)

data.title: European Bond Markets Focus on Prospect of Higher Defense Spending — Market Talk
data.description: The main story in the European fixed income market is the increase in public spending on defense and aid to Ukraine, says Danske Bank Research's August Hyldgaard in a note. The European Union is looking into various options to fund the increased spending through either joint financing or suspending the EU fiscal rules, similar to that during the Covid-19 crisis, the analyst says. "The response in the bond market was higher yields across the EGBs [eurozone government bonds], but with tighter spreads between periphery and semi-core EGBs relative to core EGBs," he says. This means that there isn't much flight to quality despite the higher yields and the pressure on fiscal policy, he says. The 10-year Bund yield rises 0.5 basis point to 2.488%, according to LSEG data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Sivers Semiconductors Appoints Alexander McCann as Strategic Senior Advisor
data.description:
Sivers Semiconductors Appoints Alexander McCann as Strategic Senior Advisor

PR Newswire

KISTA, Sweden, Feb. 18, 2025

Semiconductor leader brings more than two decades of executive leadership expertise with a primary focus in Global Operations, Manufacturing and Supply Chain.

KISTA, Sweden, Feb. 18, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Sivers Semiconductors AB (STO: SIVE), a global leader in photonics and wireless technologies, today announced the appointment of Alexander McCann as a strategic Senior Advisor to the Board and the CEO. In this role, Mr. McCann's primary focus will be on strengthening and scaling the company's photonics operations.

Mr. McCann joins Sivers Semiconductors with more than 25 years of executive leadership experience in the semiconductor industry. He has played a key role in driving world-class global operations and supply chain strategies for leading semiconductor companies, including Linear Technology Corp., Analog Devices Inc., and Dialog Semiconductor Ltd.

"We are thrilled to welcome Alex to the Sivers team. His deep expertise in operations and supply chain management, along with his extensive industry network are invaluable at this stage for our company," said Vickram Vathulya, CEO of Sivers Semiconductors. "Alex has a proven track record of crafting and deploying operations strategies with successful outcomes, and we look forward to leverage his leadership towards our continued expansion and success in the photonics market."

Mr. McCann was formerly the Senior Vice President of Global Operations at Dialog Semiconductor, where he oversaw global operations and supply chain management. During his tenure, he was part of the executive team that successfully executed Dialog's $6 billion sale to Renesas Electronics in 2021.

Prior to joining Dialog Semiconductor, Mr. McCann served as Vice President of Global Operations of Analog Devices where he led all operational functions including global multi-site manufacturing, engineering, facilities, customer service, supply chain functions and engineering support. Before joining Analog Devices, Mr. McCann also served as Chief Operating Officer at Linear Technology Corp for more than a decade. During his time as COO, he helped successfully execute a $14 billion sale to Analog Devices in 2017.

"I'm excited to join Sivers Semiconductors as a Senior Advisor and contribute to the growth of its photonics business," said McCann. "Sivers has a very credible strategic plan and is at the forefront of innovation in photonics. I look forward to working with Vickram and the team, applying my experience in operations and supply chain management to help scale the business and capitalize on the expanding market opportunities."

Mr. McCann holds a Master of Business Administration (with Distinction) from the University of Glasgow Business School. In addition to his new role with Sivers Semiconductors, Mr. McCann serves as President of Fortan Advisors and is a Semiconductor Partner at Alexa Capital. He also sits on the boards of LUMEOVA and Probe Test Solutions Ltd (PTSL).

About Sivers Semiconductors

Sivers Semiconductors is a critical enabler of a greener data economy with energy-efficient photonics & wireless solutions. Our differentiated high-precision laser and RF beamformer technologies help our customers in key markets such as AI datacenters, SATCOM, Defense and Telecom solve essential performance challenges while enabling a much greener footprint. For additional information, please visit us at: www.sivers-semiconductors.com (SIVE.ST)

data.title: UK Total Pay Growth at 13-Month High
data.description:
Average weekly earnings, including bonuses, in the UK rose by 6.0% year-on-year to GBP 710/week in the three months to December 2024, marking the highest increase in 13 months.

This followed a 5.5% rise in the previous period and slightly exceeded market forecasts of 5.9%.

Pay growth accelerated in both the private sector (6.3% vs. 5.8%) and the public sector (4.8% vs. 4.2%).

Adjusted for inflation, total earnings increased by 2.5%, up from 2.4% in the three months to November.

data.title: Plus500 to Begin $110 Million Share Buyback Program
data.description:
Trading platforms operator Plus500 plans to commence a new share buyback program totaling $110 million.

The company said in a Tuesday filing that the program will commence immediately after its existing $110 million buyback program is completed. The new program is slated to end by Dec. 31.

data.title: EDA Group Launches Share Award Scheme
data.description:
EDA Group's board has approved the adoption of a share award plan to reward and incentivize certain participants for the growth of the company, a Monday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

Participants of the share award scheme, which is valid for 10 years, include employees, related entities, and service providers, according to the supply chain service provider.

data.title: UK Employment Up 107K in December
data.description:
The number of employed people in the United Kingdom jumped by 107 thousand in the three months to December 2024, following a 36 thousand increase in the previous period.

This marks the ninth consecutive quarter of job growth.

The employment rate for those aged 16 to 64 edged up to 74.9%, 0.1 percentage point higher than the prior period.

On an annual basis, employment rose by 479 thousand, driven by growth in both full-time and part-time jobs, while self-employment declined.

data.title: UK Payrolls Up 21K in January
data.description:
The number of payrolled employees in the United Kingdom increased by 21 thousand, or 0.1% to 30.4 million in January 2025, following a revised 14 thousand decline in the previous month.

Compared to the same month last year, payrolled employment remained broadly stable, showing a rise of 49 thousand.

The largest annual increase in employment was observed in the health and social work sector (+92 thousand), while the accommodation and food service activities sector saw the biggest drop (-58 thousand).

Across regions, the highest annual growth in payrolled employment was recorded in Orkney Islands (2.8%), whereas Westminster experienced the largest fall (-2.3%).

Meanwhile, the median monthly pay rose by 5.7% year-on-year to £2,467.

The accommodation and food service activities sector posted the highest pay growth (10.4%), while the finance and insurance sector had the smallest increase (2.9%).

data.title: U.K Competition Watchdog Probes Safran, Collins Aerospace Deal
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

The U.K competition watchdog launched an investigation into French aerospace-industry supplier Safran's proposed purchase of a part of Collins Aerospace's actuation and flight control business.

The Competition and Markets Authority said Tuesday that it was considering whether the merger, if carried into effect, would result in a lessening of competition within any market in the U.K. for goods or services.

The regulator set a deadline of April 16 for its phase 1 decision.

Safran couldn't immediately be reached for comment.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: Property Firm Assura Rejects KKR's $1.97 Billion Takeover Offer
data.description:
By Michael Susin

Assura said its board unanimously rejected the nonbinding takeover offer from KKR, which valued the company at 1.56 billion pounds ($1.97 billion).

The London-listed property business on Tuesday said the deal materially undervalued the company and its prospects.

"The board remains confident in the long-term prospects of the company and believes that Assura is strongly positioned to create value for shareholders," the company said.

No further proposal from KKR has been received, it added.

The offer, which represented a 28% premium on the closing price of 48 pence a share on Feb. 13, was the fourth indicative nonbinding proposal from KKR to the board of Assura over the last six months.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0726 GMT - Aztech Global's order book may recover in 2026, CGS International's William Tng says in a research report. A key customer for the electronics-product manufacturer may still need some time to digest excess inventories before a potential order rebound in 2026, the analyst says. The company will also likely continue efforts to achieve customer diversification, Tng says, noting the company has been engaging potential and current customers in new product activities. The brokerage upgrades the stock to add from reduce as a recent order-book slowdown has probably been priced in. CGS raises the target price to S$0.82 from S$0.78. Shares are 5.8% higher at S$0.73. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0711 GMT - Chinese shares closed lower positive sentiment over President Xi's meeting with tech CEOs and the recent Chinese AI hype took a back seat. Investors are watching for the "Two Sessions" meeting in early March to evaluate Beijing's economic growth plan for this year. Semiconductor and technology stocks led the gains. Will Semiconductor closed 5.4% higher and Shengyi Technology rose 4.6%. Meanwhile consumption and media stocks led losses, with Mango Excellent Media down 6.9%. Oppein Home lost 6.0% and Focus Media Information Technology fell 3.9%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index closed 0.9% lower at 3324.49 and the Shenzhen Composite Index fell 2.0%. The ChiNext Price Index ended 2.0% lower.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0655 GMT - Nordic markets are seen opening slightly higher, with IG calling the OMXS30 up 0.1% at around 2746. Leaders of major European economies met in Paris last night and confirmed the need for Europe to increase its defense spending, SEB says in a note. Defense spending will be excluded from the EU's budgetary rules, but given the debt situation in many economies, joint borrowing in some form cannot be ruled out, SEB adds. U.S. markets were closed yesterday but European stock markets rose, largely driven by the defense sector. Stock market futures are currently pointing upwards in the U.S. and a little more sideways in Europe. Geopolitics probably gets the most attention today, as representatives of the U.S. and Russia meet in Saudi Arabia, SEB adds. The OMXS30 closed at 2743.26, OMXN40 at 2609.03 and OBX at 1409.09. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0655 GMT - Samvardhana Motherson International's growth may be supported by some tailwinds, Nomura analysts say in a research report as they maintain the stock's buy rating. Availability of Japanese technology along with acquisitions will be leveraged worldwide, based on commentary from the automotive components manufacturer's management. Also, management anticipates very strong ramp-up in the Indian company's consumer electronics plant from 4Q FY 2025, with two larger plants coming online in FY 2026-2027, the analysts note. However, Nomura cuts its target price-to-earnings ratio for the company to 18x from 22x to reflect risks from global uncertainty over tariffs affecting demand. It lowers the stock's target price to INR155.00 from INR209.00. Shares are 1.6% higher at INR126.90. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0647 GMT - South Korean shares gained for a sixth straight session, with the benchmark Kospi closing 0.6% higher at 2626.81. Foreign and institutional investors were net buyers. Index heavyweight Samsung Electronics rose 1.6% following its announcement to cancel around KRW3 trillion of shares. Howitzer and aircraft-engine maker Hanwha Aerospace climbed 11%, extending solid gains after showcasing its long-range surface-to-air missile system at this week's exhibition in Abu Dhabi. Most local defense stocks were higher, with guided missile manufacturer LIG Nex1 rising 9.1%. USD/KRW settled 0.1% higher at 1,443.70 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield was down 0.3 bps at 2.866%.(kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)While no regulatory action is required, and corrective measures have been taken, recent fines for prolonged service disruptions and the CFO's departure could collectively raise Maybank's reputational risk and hurt investors' confidence, TA Securities analyst Li Hsia Wong says. "Maybank CFO's Departure May Damp Investors' Confidence — Market Talk," at 0432 GMT, incorrectly said that no corrective measures have been taken.

0549 GMT - Thai Union Group's growth could be curbed by higher costs, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Tanida Jirapornkasemsuk says in a research report. The seafood products producer's selling, general and administrative expenses-to-sales ratio will probably increase this year owing to higher transformation costs and marketing expenses aimed at boosting sales of branded products, the analyst says. Also, the increase in the company's effective tax rate to 15% this year from 7.5% last year due to implementation of the global minimum tax scheme may drag down its 2025 core EPS. The brokerage lowers the stock's target price to THB12.00 from THB13.60 with an unchanged hold rating. Shares are 1.7% higher at THB12.10. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0530 GMT - Tencent's move to integrate DeepSeek into its messaging app will likely help WeChat's long-term growth potential, according to Morningstar analyst Ivan Su. The move suggests that Tencent is prioritizing functionality and user experience over exclusivity, Su says in a research note. While the development won't boost earnings immediately, it strengthens WeChat's role in the daily lives of the 1.3 billion app users, building its intangible-asset moat source and positioning it for long-term monetization growth through greater usage, the analyst says. Despite the recent rally, he notes that Tencent's stock still trades at a roughly 30% discount to Morningstar's estimate of its fair value, which is maintained at HK$704.00. Shares are 3.3% higher at HK$510.00. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.description: Aztech Global's order book may recover in 2026, CGS International's William Tng says in a research report. A key customer for the electronics-product manufacturer may still need some time to digest excess inventories before a potential order rebound in 2026, the analyst says. The company will also likely continue efforts to achieve customer diversification, Tng says, noting the company has been engaging potential and current customers in new product activities. The brokerage upgrades the stock to add from reduce as a recent order-book slowdown has probably been priced in. CGS raises the target price to S$0.82 from S$0.78. Shares are 5.8% higher at S$0.73. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Juneyao Airlines' Passenger Capacity Rises 19% in January
data.description:
Juneyao Airlines' passenger capacity jumped 19% year on year to about 5.39 billion available seat kilometers in January, according to a Tuesday filing with the Shanghai Stock Exchange.

Total passenger count grew 6.5% to 2.4 million during the month.

Cargo and mail capacity increased 34% to 172.3 million available freight-tonne kilometers.

Total cargo and mail carried rose 27% to 14,305.2 tons, the filing said.

Shares slipped 2% during Tuesday's afternoon trading.

data.title: UK Wages Rise as Expected
data.description:
Regular pay in the UK, excluding bonuses, increased by 5.9% year-on-year to GBP 664/week in the three months to December 2024, accelerating from 5.6% in the previous period and matching market consensus.

It was the fastest rise in eight months, with wage growth quickening in both the private sector (6.2% vs. 5.9%) and the public sector (4.7% vs. 4.2%).

Wholesale, retailing, hotels, and restaurants saw the most significant increase (6.6%), followed by construction (6.5%), manufacturing (6%), services (6%), and finance and business services (5.9%).

Adjusted for inflation, wage growth in real terms rose by 2.5%, the highest in seven months, up from the prior figure of 2.4%.

data.title: IMotion Automotive Technology Raises HK$228 Million via Share Placement
data.description:
IMotion Automotive Technology (Suzhou) raised HK$228.4 million in net proceeds by placing 1,190,200 shares to at least six investors at HK$20.88 apiece, a Monday filing with Hong Kong Exchange said.

The shares represent 4.63% of the Chinese autonomous driving solution company's enlarged issued share capital.

The company will use the proceeds from the issue to boost its R&D of certain solutions and products, to expand its overseas sales and service network, and for working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: The Hour Glass Repurchases More Shares
data.description:
The Hour Glass bought back 5,600 shares in the open market on Monday for SG$8,784 or SG$1.56 apiece, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The luxury watch retail group has been authorized to buy back 64.8 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has bought back 518,300 shares.

data.title: UK Unemployment Rate Steady at 4.4% in Quarter to December 2024
data.description:
Britain's unemployment rate came in at 4.4% in the quarter to December 2024, unchanged from the prior three-month period, the Office for National Statistics said Tuesday.

The latest reading is below the consensus estimate of 4.5%.

Meanwhile, the UK employment rate came in at 74.9%.

data.title: Ad-Manum Finance's CFO Resigns
data.description: Ad-Manum Finance said its chief financial officer, Vikas Gupta, has resigned from the company, effective Feb. 15, according to a Tuesday filing to the BSE.

data.title: IIFL Finance to Raise Up to INR1 Billion Via Debt Offering; Shares Down 4%
data.description:
IIFL Finance has received board approval to raise up to 1 billion Indian rupees through the allotment of non-convertible debentures on private placement basis, according to a Tuesday filing with the Indian stock exchanges.

The company will allot 100 unsecured, redeemable, non-convertible, subordinated debentures of the face value of 10 million rupees each.

The debentures will carry a coupon rate of 9.5% per annum and will mature on Oct. 16, 2034. They will be listed on the National Stock Exchange of India.

Shares of the company were down 4% in recent trade.

data.title: UK Claimant Count Jumps in January
data.description:
The number of people claiming unemployment-related benefits in Britain rose by 22,000 in January, against the revised decrease of 15,100 in the prior month, according to data from the Office for National Statistics published Tuesday.

Analysts expected a 10,000 increase for the month.
